sections.paragraph: (from the root קָרָה , of the form כֶּסֶת, from כָּסָה), fem. a poet. word, i.q. קִרְיָה a city, but of less frequent occurrence, Job 29:7 Pro 8:3 9:3 11:11 . Ch. קַרְתָּא id. The same word is found in Persic and Syriac names of cities, as Cirta, Tigranocerta, also on the Phœnicio-Sicilian coins struck at Panorruns [Palermo], which have on the face the inscription מחנת (מַחֲנֹת the camp, prob. a domestic name of the city), on the back קרת חדשת (קֶרֶת חֲדֶשֶׁת New City), prob. Carthage, to which Panormus was subject; see Bayer, on Sallust. p. 347; Mionnet, Descr. des Médailles, plate 20; [also Monumm. Phœn.]
